instruction: The repository audit spec at ~/Desktop/scan_spec.json tells you how the knowledge base under /tmp/knowledge_base/ should be summarized. Use the terminal to inspect that folder and update ~/Desktop/repo_audit/project_meta.json with the actual counts for pythonFiles and markdownFiles, and set status to audited. Then apply the tabSize and ruler values from the spec to VS Code User Settings. Finally, open the docsUrl from the spec in Chrome.

parameters.command:
python3 - <<'PY'
import json
from pathlib import Path
base = Path('/tmp/knowledge_base')
(base / 'notes').mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
(base / 'src').mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
(base / 'src' / 'a.py').write_text('print(1)\n')
(base / 'src' / 'b.py').write_text('print(2)\n')
(base / 'src' / 'c.py').write_text('print(3)\n')
(base / 'README.md').write_text('# kb\n')
(base / 'notes' / 'plan.md').write_text('todo\n')
Path('/home/user/Desktop/repo_audit').mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
json.dump({'pythonFiles': 0, 'markdownFiles': 0, 'status': 'pending'}, open('/home/user/Desktop/repo_audit/project_meta.json', 'w'), indent=2)
json.dump({'tabSize': 4, 'ruler': 90, 'docsUrl': 'https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/editor/codebasics'}, open('/home/user/Desktop/scan_spec.json', 'w'), indent=2)
PY
